justification: Fully idiomatic Jac `obj` archetype: `has`-declared fields with type annotations, methods defined via `def` (not Python `class` / `__init__`), implicit `self` in method signatures. Matches jac://guide/patterns §2 (Archetype with has declarations) and jac://guide/pitfalls §4, §5, §5b, §10, §13 (use obj not class; def for methods; self implicit; has for fields; type annotations required).

Fully idiomatic graph construction: typed `node City` + typed `edge Road` with a `has distance: float` payload, connect operator `+>:Road(distance=...):+>` for typed-edge creation, full dict/tuple type annotations on params and return. Matches jac://guide/patterns §4, §5 (node/edge archetypes, typed edges) and jac://guide/pitfalls §18, §19, §20 (edge definitions, graph construction, typed edge syntax).

Fully idiomatic walker: typed-node entry ability (`can check with User entry`), `visit [-->]` for traversal, `disengage` for early termination on match, `jid(here)`-keyed `set[str]` visited-dedupe (mandatory per docs/findings/jac-pitfalls.md §Walkers `visit` does not auto-dedupe). Matches jac://guide/patterns §4 (Walker Definition and Traversal) and jac://guide/pitfalls §17 (walker + typed-node entry + visit).

Mostly idiomatic: uses node-side ability `can tick with ViewBumper entry` (idiomatic per docs/findings/jac-pitfalls.md §`here` is not valid in node-side abilities — uses `self` and `visitor` correctly), walker dedupe via `jid(self)` in `visitor.visited`, typed edges via `[-->]`. Minor issue: `has views: int` lacks a default (other tasks' nodes use `has views: int = 0` to make construction default-safe), a small violation of jac://guide/pitfalls §13 (type annotations with defaults for has fields).

Mostly idiomatic: typed node+edge archetypes, typed-edge connect operator, index-aligned `[edge city ->:Road:->]` filter for per-edge mutation. Minor smell: iterates `for i in range(len(edges))` and assigns to `edges[i].distance` when every edge in the selection receives the same value — the bulk spatial-assign idiom `[edge city -->][?:Road](=distance=new_distance)` from docs/findings/jac-pitfalls.md §Spatial assign comprehension would be more idiomatic for blanket mutation.

Mostly idiomatic walker: typed-node `can stop with City entry`, visit + jid-keyed set dedupe. Minor smell: the generic `can kickoff with entry` only fires at the spawn node and does not continue traversal (per docs/findings/jac-pitfalls.md §Generic `can ... with entry` only fires at the spawn location) — the more idiomatic form is `can kickoff with Root entry { visit [-->]; }` or removing the bootstrapping ability entirely and relying on the typed `with City entry`.

Correct and uses walker+visit+typed-ability, but the visited-tracker is a `list[str]` with linear-scan membership testing via `for v in self.visited` instead of the idiomatic `set[str]` with O(1) `in` (established pattern in all walker tasks 07–10 and jac://guide/patterns §4). This is Python-transliterated data structure choice in otherwise correct walker code. Violates the spirit of jac://guide/pitfalls §13 (pick typed Jac-idiomatic state shapes, not Python-reflexive ones).

Correct: typed node + edge archetypes with `++>` connect operator. Not idiomatic: maintains a parallel `adj: dict[str, list[str]]` adjacency that duplicates the graph-native relationships — the Jac runtime already stores the connectivity on the nodes. Violates the spirit of jac://guide/pitfalls §18, §19 and fixtures/python_ish/uses_connect_op.jac (dict-of-lists is the canonical Python-transliterated anti-pattern) — this snippet straddles the line, keeping the graph but wrapping it in dict indirection.

Correct but not idiomatic: walker terminates via a `done: bool` flag + bare `return` where `disengage` is the Jac-native primitive for aborting walker traversal. Every subsequent node fires the ability just to hit `if self.done { return; }`, which `disengage` would short-circuit cleanly. Violates jac://guide/patterns §4 and the canonical FindAdmin pattern in tasks/walker/08/solution.jac (disengage is the intended form). Walker+visit+typed-node are correctly used, which keeps this above tier 2.

Python-transliterated: no `node`, no `edge`, no `walker`, no connect operator, no `visit`. Graph is a dict-of-lists adjacency with a parallel dict-of-dicts distances table — a textbook Python pattern. Directly matches the canonical anti-pattern in tests/fixtures/python_ish/uses_connect_op.jac and violates jac://guide/pitfalls §18, §19, §20 (edges are first-class archetypes with connect operators, not dict keys).

Python-transliterated: archetype exists but traversal is hand-rolled as `while len(frontier) > 0` BFS with `frontier.pop(0)` and manual `seen` set over `[cur -->]`. No walker, no `visit`. Directly matches tests/fixtures/python_ish/uses_walker.jac and violates jac://guide/pitfalls §17 (Walker definition and visit syntax — traversal should use walker+visit, not manual frontier loops).

Python-transliterated: `node TreeNode` is shaped like a Python class with recursion through `[self -->]` inside a regular `def traverse` method. No walker, no `visit`, no ability — traversal is object-oriented recursion. Violates jac://guide/pitfalls §5 (`can` with typed entry is the mechanism for traversal behavior on nodes, `def` on a node is for ordinary methods) and jac://guide/pitfalls §17 (walker + visit is the graph-traversal primitive).

Not valid Jac — plain Python syntax (colon-indentation blocks, `class`, `__init__`, `if __name__ == "__main__":`, no semicolons). Fails parsing with 'Missing ;', 'Unexpected token :' per validate_jac. Violates jac://guide/pitfalls §1 (semicolons required), §2 (braces not indentation), §4 (prefer obj over class), §6 (`def init`, not `def __init__`), §5b (self implicit — Python's explicit `self` is wrong).

Not valid Jac — plain English prose, no code tokens. Fails at the first identifier-shaped English word per validate_jac ('Unexpected token This'). Not parseable; violates every syntactic requirement in jac://guide/pitfalls §1–§25.

Not valid Jac — gibberish with stray `@@@`, `!!!`, unbalanced `{{{`, invalid operators like `<--!>`, meaningless identifiers. Fails parse with 'Unexpected character' and 'Unexpected token' errors throughout. Not parseable; violates jac://guide/pitfalls §1, §2, §17 (and basic lexical sanity).
